NKJV.1: Finally, my brethren, rejoice in the Lord. For me to write the same things to you is not tedious, but for you it is safe.
NKJV.2: Beware of dogs, beware of evil workers, beware of the mutilation!
NKJV.3: For we are the circumcision, who worship God in the Spirit, rejoice in Christ Jesus, and have no confidence in the flesh,
NKJV.4: though I also might have confidence in the flesh. If anyone else thinks he may have confidence in the flesh, I more so:
NKJV.5: circumcised the eighth day, of the stock of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew of the Hebrews; concerning the law, a Pharisee;
NKJV.6: concerning zeal, persecuting the church; concerning the righteousness which is in the law, blameless.
NKJV.7: But what things were gain to me, these I have counted loss for Christ.
NKJV.8: Yet indeed I also count all things loss for the excellence of the knowledge of Christ Jesus my Lord, for whom I have suffered the loss of all things, and count them as rubbish, that I may gain Christ
NKJV.9: and be found in Him, not having my own righteousness, which is from the law, but that which is through faith in Christ, the righteousness which is from God by faith;
NKJV.10: that I may know Him and the power of His resurrection, and the fellowship of His sufferings, being conformed to His death,
NKJV.11: if, by any means, I may attain to the resurrection from the dead.
NKJV.12: Not that I have already attained, or am already perfected; but I press on, that I may lay hold of that for which Christ Jesus has also laid hold of me.
NKJV.13: Brethren, I do not count myself to have apprehended; but one thing I do, forgetting those things which are behind and reaching forward to those things which are ahead,
NKJV.14: I press toward the goal for the prize of the upward call of God in Christ Jesus.
NKJV.15: Therefore let us, as many as are mature, have this mind; and if in anything you think otherwise, God will reveal even this to you.
NKJV.16: Nevertheless, to the degree that we have already attained, let us walk by the same rule, let us be of the same mind.
NKJV.17: Brethren, join in following my example, and note those who so walk, as you have us for a pattern.
NKJV.18: For many walk, of whom I have told you often, and now tell you even weeping, that they are the enemies of the cross of Christ:
NKJV.19: whose end is destruction, whose god is their belly, and whose glory is in their shame— who set their mind on earthly things.
NKJV.20: For our citizenship is in heaven, from which we also eagerly wait for the Savior, the Lord Jesus Christ,
NKJV.21: who will transform our lowly body that it may be conformed to His glorious body, according to the working by which He is able even to subdue all things to Himself.

ESV.1: Finally, my brothers,  rejoice in the Lord. To write the same things to you is no trouble to me and is safe for you.
ESV.2: Look out for the dogs, look out for the evildoers, look out for those who mutilate the flesh.
ESV.3: For we are the circumcision, who worship by the Spirit of God  and glory in Christ Jesus and put no confidence in the flesh —
ESV.4: though I myself have reason for confidence in the flesh also. If anyone else thinks he has reason for confidence in the flesh, I have more:
ESV.5: circumcised on the eighth day, of the people of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew of Hebrews; as to the law, a Pharisee;
ESV.6: as to zeal, a persecutor of the church; as to righteousness under the law,  blameless.
ESV.7: But whatever gain I had, I counted as loss for the sake of Christ.
ESV.8: Indeed, I count everything as loss because of the surpassing worth of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord. For his sake I have suffered the loss of all things and count them as rubbish, in order that I may gain Christ
ESV.9: and be found in him, not having a righteousness of my own that comes from the law, but that which comes through faith in Christ, the righteousness from God that depends on faith —
ESV.10: that I may know him and the power of his resurrection, and may share his sufferings, becoming like him in his death,
ESV.11: that by any means possible I may attain the resurrection from the dead.
ESV.12: Not that I have already obtained this or am already perfect, but I press on to make it my own, because Christ Jesus has made me his own.
ESV.13: Brothers, I do not consider that I have made it my own. But one thing I do: forgetting what lies behind and straining forward to what lies ahead,
ESV.14: I press on toward the goal for the prize of the upward call of God in Christ Jesus.
ESV.15: Let those of us who are mature think this way, and if in anything you think otherwise, God will reveal that also to you.
ESV.16: Only let us hold true to what we have attained.
ESV.17: Brothers, join in imitating me, and keep your eyes on those who walk according to the example you have in us.
ESV.18: For many, of whom I have often told you and now tell you even with tears, walk as enemies of the cross of Christ.
ESV.19: Their end is destruction, their god is their belly, and they glory in their shame, with minds set on earthly things.
ESV.20: But our citizenship is in heaven, and from it we await a Savior, the Lord Jesus Christ,
ESV.21: who will transform our lowly body to be like his glorious body, by the power that enables him even to subject all things to himself.

NASB.1: Finally, my brethren, rejoice in the Lord. To write the same things [again] is no trouble to me, and it is a safeguard for you.
NASB.2: Beware of the dogs, beware of the evil workers, beware of the false circumcision;
NASB.3: for we are the [true] circumcision, who worship in the Spirit of God and glory in Christ Jesus and put no confidence in the flesh,
NASB.4: although I myself might have confidence even in the flesh. If anyone else has a mind to put confidence in the flesh, I far more:
NASB.5: circumcised the eighth day, of the nation of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew of Hebrews; as to the Law, a Pharisee;
NASB.6: as to zeal, a persecutor of the church; as to the righteousness which is in the Law, found blameless.
NASB.7: But whatever things were gain to me, those things I have counted as loss for the sake of Christ.
NASB.8: More than that, I count all things to be loss in view of the surpassing value of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord, for whom I have suffered the loss of all things, and count them but rubbish so that I may gain Christ,
NASB.9: and may be found in Him, not having a righteousness of my own derived from [the] Law, but that which is through faith in Christ, the righteousness which [comes] from God on the basis of faith,
NASB.10: that I may know Him and the power of His resurrection and the fellowship of His sufferings, being conformed to His death;
NASB.11: in order that I may attain to the resurrection from the dead.
NASB.12: Not that I have already obtained [it] or have already become perfect, but I press on so that I may lay hold of that for which also I was laid hold of by Christ Jesus.
NASB.13: Brethren, I do not regard myself as having laid hold of [it] yet; but one thing [I do]: forgetting what [lies] behind and reaching forward to what [lies] ahead,
NASB.14: I press on toward the goal for the prize of the upward call of God in Christ Jesus.
NASB.15: Let us therefore, as many as are perfect, have this attitude; and if in anything you have a different attitude, God will reveal that also to you;
NASB.16: however, let us keep living by that same [standard] to which we have attained.
NASB.17: Brethren, join in following my example, and observe those who walk according to the pattern you have in us.
NASB.18: For many walk, of whom I often told you, and now tell you even weeping, [that they are] enemies of the cross of Christ,
NASB.19: whose end is destruction, whose god is [their] appetite, and [whose] glory is in their shame, who set their minds on earthly things.
NASB.20: For our citizenship is in heaven, from which also we eagerly wait for a Savior, the Lord Jesus Christ;
NASB.21: who will transform the body of our humble state into conformity with the body of His glory, by the exertion of the power that He has even to subject all things to Himself.

NLT.1: Whatever happens, my dear brothers and sisters, rejoice in the Lord. I never get tired of telling you these things, and I do it to safeguard your faith.
NLT.2: Watch out for those dogs, those people who do evil, those mutilators who say you must be circumcised to be saved.
NLT.3: For we who worship by the Spirit of God are the ones who are truly circumcised. We rely on what Christ Jesus has done for us. We put no confidence in human effort,
NLT.4: though I could have confidence in my own effort if anyone could. Indeed, if others have reason for confidence in their own efforts, I have even more!
NLT.5: I was circumcised when I was eight days old. I am a pure-blooded citizen of Israel and a member of the tribe of Benjamin — a real Hebrew if there ever was one! I was a member of the Pharisees, who demand the strictest obedience to the Jewish law.
NLT.6: I was so zealous that I harshly persecuted the church. And as for righteousness, I obeyed the law without fault.
NLT.7: I once thought these things were valuable, but now I consider them worthless because of what Christ has done.
NLT.8: Yes, everything else is worthless when compared with the infinite value of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord. For his sake I have discarded everything else, counting it all as garbage, so that I could gain Christ
NLT.9: and become one with him. I no longer count on my own righteousness through obeying the law; rather, I become righteous through faith in Christ. For God’s way of making us right with himself depends on faith.
NLT.10: I want to know Christ and experience the mighty power that raised him from the dead. I want to suffer with him, sharing in his death,
NLT.11: so that one way or another I will experience the resurrection from the dead!
NLT.12: I don’t mean to say that I have already achieved these things or that I have already reached perfection. But I press on to possess that perfection for which Christ Jesus first possessed me.
NLT.13: No, dear brothers and sisters, I have not achieved it, but I focus on this one thing: Forgetting the past and looking forward to what lies ahead,
NLT.14: I press on to reach the end of the race and receive the heavenly prize for which God, through Christ Jesus, is calling us.
NLT.15: Let all who are spiritually mature agree on these things. If you disagree on some point, I believe God will make it plain to you.
NLT.16: But we must hold on to the progress we have already made.
NLT.17: Dear brothers and sisters, pattern your lives after mine, and learn from those who follow our example.
NLT.18: For I have told you often before, and I say it again with tears in my eyes, that there are many whose conduct shows they are really enemies of the cross of Christ.
NLT.19: They are headed for destruction. Their god is their appetite, they brag about shameful things, and they think only about this life here on earth.
NLT.20: But we are citizens of heaven, where the Lord Jesus Christ lives. And we are eagerly waiting for him to return as our Savior.
NLT.21: He will take our weak mortal bodies and change them into glorious bodies like his own, using the same power with which he will bring everything under his control.

WEB.1: Finally, my brothers, rejoice in the Lord. To write the same things to you, to me indeed is not tiresome, but for you it is safe.
WEB.2: Beware of the dogs, beware of the evil workers, beware of the false circumcision.
WEB.3: For we are the circumcision, who worship God in the Spirit, and rejoice in Christ Jesus, and have no confidence in the flesh;
WEB.4: though I myself might have confidence even in the flesh. If any other man thinks that he has confidence in the flesh, I yet more:
WEB.5: circumcised the eighth day, of the stock of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew of Hebrews; concerning the law, a Pharisee;
WEB.6: concerning zeal, persecuting the assembly; concerning the righteousness which is in the law, found blameless.
WEB.7: However, I consider those things that were gain to me as a loss for Christ.
WEB.8: Yes most certainly, and I count all things to be a loss for the excellency of the knowledge of Christ Jesus, my Lord, for whom I suffered the loss of all things, and count them nothing but refuse, that I may gain Christ
WEB.9: and be found in him, not having a righteousness of my own, that which is of the law, but that which is through faith in Christ, the righteousness which is from God by faith;
WEB.10: that I may know him, and the power of his resurrection, and the fellowship of his sufferings, becoming conformed to his death;
WEB.11: if by any means I may attain to the resurrection from the dead.
WEB.12: Not that I have already obtained, or am already made perfect; but I press on, if it is so that I may take hold of that for which also I was taken hold of by Christ Jesus.
WEB.13: Brothers, I don’t regard myself as yet having taken hold, but one thing I do. Forgetting the things which are behind, and stretching forward to the things which are before,
WEB.14: I press on toward the goal for the prize of the high calling of God in Christ Jesus.
WEB.15: Let us therefore, as many as are perfect, think this way. If in anything you think otherwise, God will also reveal that to you.
WEB.16: Nevertheless, to the extent that we have already attained, let us walk by the same rule. Let us be of the same mind.
WEB.17: Brothers, be imitators together of me, and note those who walk this way, even as you have us for an example.
WEB.18: For many walk, of whom I told you often, and now tell you even weeping, as the enemies of the cross of Christ,
WEB.19: whose end is destruction, whose god is the belly, and whose glory is in their shame, who think about earthly things.
WEB.20: For our citizenship is in heaven, from where we also wait for a Savior, the Lord Jesus Christ;
WEB.21: who will change the body of our humiliation to be conformed to the body of his glory, according to the working by which he is able even to subject all things to himself.

CSB17.1: In addition, my brothers and sisters, rejoice <sup>ⓗ</sup> in the Lord. To write to you again about this is no trouble for me and is a safeguard for you.
CSB17.2: Watch out for the dogs, watch out for the evil <sup>ⓘ</sup> workers, watch out for those who mutilate the flesh.
CSB17.3: For we are the circumcision, <sup>ⓙ</sup> the ones who worship by the Spirit <sup>ⓚ</sup> of God, boast in Christ Jesus, <sup>ⓛ</sup> and do not put confidence in the flesh —
CSB17.4: although I have reasons for confidence in the flesh. If anyone else thinks he has grounds for confidence in the flesh, I have more:
CSB17.5: circumcised the eighth day; <sup>ⓜ</sup> of the nation of Israel, <sup>ⓝ</sup> of the tribe of Benjamin, <sup>ⓐ</sup> a Hebrew born of Hebrews; regarding the law, <sup>ⓑ</sup> a Pharisee;
CSB17.6: regarding zeal, <sup>ⓒ</sup> persecuting <sup>ⓓ</sup> the church; regarding the righteousness that is in the law, <sup>ⓔ</sup> blameless. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.7: But everything that was a gain to me, I have considered to be a loss because of Christ.
CSB17.8: More than that, I also consider everything to be a loss in view of the surpassing value <sup>ⓖ</sup> of knowing Christ <sup>ⓗ</sup> Jesus my Lord. Because of him I have suffered the loss of all things and consider them as dung, so that I may gain Christ <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.9: and be found in him, not having a righteousness of my own from the law, <sup>ⓙ</sup> but one that is through faith in Christ <sup>[1]</sup>  — the righteousness from God based on faith. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.10: My goal is to know him and the power of his resurrection and the fellowship of his sufferings, <sup>ⓛ</sup> being conformed to his death, <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.11: assuming that I will somehow reach the resurrection from among the dead.
CSB17.12: Not that I have already reached the goal or am already perfect, but I make every effort to take hold <sup>ⓝ</sup> of it because I also have been taken hold of by Christ Jesus.
CSB17.13: Brothers and sisters, I do not <sup>[2]</sup> consider myself to have taken hold of it. But one thing I do: Forgetting what is behind <sup>ⓐ</sup> and reaching forward to what is ahead,
CSB17.14: I pursue as my goal the prize promised by God’s heavenly <sup>[3]</sup> call in Christ Jesus. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.15: Therefore, let all of us who are mature think this way. And if you think differently about anything, God will reveal <sup>ⓒ</sup> this also to you.
CSB17.16: In any case, we should live up to whatever truth we have attained.
CSB17.17: Join in imitating me, brothers and sisters, and pay careful attention to those who live according to the example you have in us.
CSB17.18: For I have often told you, and now say again with tears, that many live as enemies of the cross <sup>ⓓ</sup> of Christ.
CSB17.19: Their end is destruction; their god is their stomach; their glory <sup>ⓔ</sup> is in their shame. They are focused on earthly things,
CSB17.20: but our citizenship is in heaven, and we eagerly wait for a Savior from there, the Lord Jesus Christ.
CSB17.21: He will transform the body of our humble condition into the likeness of his glorious <sup>ⓕ</sup> body, <sup>ⓖ</sup> by the power that enables him to subject everything to himself.

AMP.1: Finally, my fellow believers, continue to rejoice and delight in the LORD. To write the same things again is no trouble for me, and it is a safeguard for you.
AMP.2: Look out for the dogs [the Judaizers, the legalists], look out for the troublemakers, look out for the false circumcision [those who claim circumcision is necessary for salvation];
AMP.3: for we [who are born-again have been reborn from above—spiritually transformed, renewed, set apart for His purpose and] are the true circumcision, who worship in the Spirit of God and glory and take pride and exult in Christ Jesus and place no confidence [in what we have or who we are] in the flesh—
AMP.4: though I myself might have [some grounds for] confidence in the flesh [if I were pursuing salvation by works]. If anyone else thinks that he has reason to be confident in the flesh [that is, in his own efforts to achieve salvation], I have far more:
AMP.5: circumcised when I was eight days old, of the nation of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew of Hebrews [an exemplary Hebrew]; as to the [observance of the] Law, a Pharisee;
AMP.6: as to my zeal [for Jewish tradition], a persecutor of the church; and as to righteousness [supposed right living] which [my fellow Jews believe] is in the Law, I proved myself blameless.
AMP.7: But whatever former things were gains to me [as I thought then], these things [once regarded as advancements in merit] I have come to consider as loss [absolutely worthless] for the sake of Christ [and the purpose which He has given my life].
AMP.8: But more than that, I count everything as loss compared to the priceless privilege and supreme advantage of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord [and of growing more deeply and thoroughly acquainted with Him—a joy unequaled]. For His sake I have lost everything, and I consider it all garbage, so that I may gain Christ,
AMP.9: and may be found in Him [believing and relying on Him], not having any righteousness of my own derived from [my obedience to] the Law and its rituals, but [possessing] that [genuine righteousness] which comes through faith in Christ, the righteousness which comes from God on the basis of faith.
AMP.10: And this, so that I may know Him [experientially, becoming more thoroughly acquainted with Him, understanding the remarkable wonders of His Person more completely] and [in that same way experience] the power of His resurrection [which overflows and is active in believers], and [that I may share] the fellowship of His sufferings, by being continually conformed [inwardly into His likeness even] to His death [dying as He did];
AMP.11: so that I may attain to the resurrection [that will raise me] from the dead.
AMP.12: Not that I have already obtained it [this goal of being Christlike] or have already been made perfect, but I actively press on so that I may take hold of that [perfection] for which Christ Jesus took hold of me and made me His own.
AMP.13: Brothers and sisters, I do not consider that I have made it my own yet; but one thing I do: forgetting what lies behind and reaching forward to what lies ahead,
AMP.14: I press on toward the goal to win the [heavenly] prize of the upward call of God in Christ Jesus.
AMP.15: All of us who are mature [pursuing spiritual perfection] should have this attitude. And if in any respect you have a different attitude, that too God will make clear to you.
AMP.16: Only let us stay true to what we have already attained.
AMP.17: Brothers and sisters, together follow my example and observe those who live by the pattern we gave you.
AMP.18: For there are many, of whom I have often told you, and now tell you even with tears, who live as enemies of the cross of Christ [rejecting and opposing His way of salvation],
AMP.19: whose fate is destruction, whose god is their belly [their worldly appetite, their sensuality, their vanity], and whose glory is in their shame—who focus their mind on earthly and temporal things.
AMP.20: But [we are different, because] our citizenship is in heaven. And from there we eagerly await [the coming of] the Savior, the Lord Jesus Christ;
AMP.21: who, by exerting that power which enables Him even to subject everything to Himself, will [not only] transform [but completely refashion] our earthly bodies so that they will be like His glorious resurrected body.

MSG.1: And that's about it, friends. Be glad in God! I don't mind repeating what I have written in earlier letters, and I hope you don't mind hearing it again. Better safe than sorry—so here goes.
MSG.2: Steer clear of the barking dogs, those religious busybodies, all bark and no bite. All they're interested in is appearances—knife-happy circumcisers, I call them.
MSG.3: The real believers are the ones the Spirit of God leads to work away at this ministry, filling the air with Christ's praise as we do it. We couldn't carry this off by our own efforts, and we know it—
MSG.4: even though we can list what many might think are impressive credentials. You know my pedigree:
MSG.5: a legitimate birth, circumcised on the eighth day; an Israelite from the elite tribe of Benjamin; a strict and devout adherent to God's law;
MSG.6: a fiery defender of the purity of my religion, even to the point of persecuting Christians; a meticulous observer of everything set down in God's law Book.
MSG.7: The very credentials these people are waving around as something special, I'm tearing up and throwing out with the trash—along with everything else I used to take credit for. And why? Because of Christ.
MSG.8: Yes, all the things I once thought were so important are gone from my life. Compared to the high privilege of knowing Christ Jesus as my Master, firsthand, everything I once thought I had going for me is insignificant—dog dung. I've dumped it all in the trash so that I could embrace Christ
MSG.9: and be embraced by him. I didn't want some petty, inferior brand of righteousness that comes from keeping a list of rules when I could get the robust kind that comes from trusting Christ—God's righteousness.
MSG.10: I gave up all that inferior stuff so I could know Christ personally, experience his resurrection power, be a partner in his suffering, and go all the way with him to death itself.
MSG.11: If there was any way to get in on the resurrection from the dead, I wanted to do it.
MSG.12: I'm not saying that I have this all together, that I have it made. But I am well on my way, reaching out for Christ, who has so wondrously reached out for me.
MSG.13: Friends, don't get me wrong: By no means do I count myself an expert in all of this, but I've got my eye on the goal, where God is beckoning us onward—to Jesus.
MSG.14: I'm off and running, and I'm not turning back.
MSG.15: So let's keep focused on that goal, those of us who want everything God has for us. If any of you have something else in mind, something less than total commitment, God will clear your blurred vision—you'll see it yet!
MSG.16: Now that we're on the right track, let's stay on it.
MSG.17: Stick with me, friends. Keep track of those you see running this same course, headed for this same goal.
MSG.18: There are many out there taking other paths, choosing other goals, and trying to get you to go along with them. I've warned you of them many times; sadly, I'm having to do it again. All they want is easy street. They hate Christ's Cross.
MSG.19: But easy street is a dead-end street. Those who live there make their bellies their gods; belches are their praise; all they can think of is their appetites.
MSG.20: But there's far more to life for us. We're citizens of high heaven! We're waiting the arrival of the Savior, the Master, Jesus Christ,
MSG.21: who will transform our earthy bodies into glorious bodies like his own. He'll make us beautiful and whole with the same powerful skill by which he is putting everything as it should be, under and around him.

NIV.1:
No Confidence in the Flesh
Finally, my brothers, rejoice in the Lord! It is no trouble for me to write the same things to you again, and it is a safeguard for you.
NIV.2: Watch out for those dogs, those men who do evil, those mutilators of the flesh.
NIV.3: For it is we who are the circumcision, we who worship by the Spirit of God, who glory in Christ Jesus, and who put no confidence in the flesh—
NIV.4:
though I myself have reasons for such confidence.
If anyone else thinks he has reasons to put confidence in the flesh, I have more:
NIV.5: circumcised on the eighth day, of the people of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew of Hebrews; in regard to the law, a Pharisee;
NIV.6: as for zeal, persecuting the church; as for legalistic righteousness, faultless.
NIV.7: But whatever was to my profit I now consider loss for the sake of Christ.
NIV.8: What is more, I consider everything a loss compared to the surpassing greatness of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord, for whose sake I have lost all things. I consider them rubbish, that I may gain Christ
NIV.9: and be found in him, not having a righteousness of my own that comes from the law, but that which is through faith in Christ—the righteousness that comes from God and is by faith.
NIV.10: I want to know Christ and the power of his resurrection and the fellowship of sharing in his sufferings, becoming like him in his death,
NIV.11: and so, somehow, to attain to the resurrection from the dead.
NIV.12:
Pressing on Toward the Goal
Not that I have already obtained all this, or have already been made perfect, but I press on to take hold of that for which Christ Jesus took hold of me.
NIV.13: Brothers, I do not consider myself yet to have taken hold of it. But one thing I do: Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead,
NIV.14: I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which God has called me heavenward in Christ Jesus.
NIV.15: All of us who are mature should take such a view of things. And if on some point you think differently, that too God will make clear to you.
NIV.16: Only let us live up to what we have already attained.
NIV.17: Join with others in following my example, brothers, and take note of those who live according to the pattern we gave you.
NIV.18: For, as I have often told you before and now say again even with tears, many live as enemies of the cross of Christ.
NIV.19: Their destiny is destruction, their god is their stomach, and their glory is in their shame. Their mind is on earthly things.
NIV.20: But our citizenship is in heaven. And we eagerly await a Savior from there, the Lord Jesus Christ,
NIV.21: who, by the power that enables him to bring everything under his control, will transform our lowly bodies so that they will be like his glorious body.

NRSVCE.1: Finally, my brothers and sisters, rejoice in the Lord. Breaking with the Past To write the same things to you is not troublesome to me, and for you it is a safeguard.
NRSVCE.2: Beware of the dogs, beware of the evil workers, beware of those who mutilate the flesh!
NRSVCE.3: For it is we who are the circumcision, who worship in the Spirit of God and boast in Christ Jesus and have no confidence in the flesh —
NRSVCE.4: even though I, too, have reason for confidence in the flesh. If anyone else has reason to be confident in the flesh, I have more:
NRSVCE.5: circumcised on the eighth day, a member of the people of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew born of Hebrews; as to the law, a Pharisee;
NRSVCE.6: as to zeal, a persecutor of the church; as to righteousness under the law, blameless.
NRSVCE.7: Yet whatever gains I had, these I have come to regard as loss because of Christ.
NRSVCE.8: More than that, I regard everything as loss because of the surpassing value of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord. For his sake I have suffered the loss of all things, and I regard them as rubbish, in order that I may gain Christ
NRSVCE.9: and be found in him, not having a righteousness of my own that comes from the law, but one that comes through faith in Christ, the righteousness from God based on faith.
NRSVCE.10: I want to know Christ and the power of his resurrection and the sharing of his sufferings by becoming like him in his death,
NRSVCE.11: if somehow I may attain the resurrection from the dead.
NRSVCE.12: Not that I have already obtained this or have already reached the goal; but I press on to make it my own, because Christ Jesus has made me his own.
NRSVCE.13: Beloved, I do not consider that I have made it my own; but this one thing I do: forgetting what lies behind and straining forward to what lies ahead,
NRSVCE.14: I press on toward the goal for the prize of the heavenly call of God in Christ Jesus.
NRSVCE.15: Let those of us then who are mature be of the same mind; and if you think differently about anything, this too God will reveal to you.
NRSVCE.16: Only let us hold fast to what we have attained.
NRSVCE.17: Brothers and sisters, join in imitating me, and observe those who live according to the example you have in us.
NRSVCE.18: For many live as enemies of the cross of Christ; I have often told you of them, and now I tell you even with tears.
NRSVCE.19: Their end is destruction; their god is the belly; and their glory is in their shame; their minds are set on earthly things.
NRSVCE.20: But our citizenship is in heaven, and it is from there that we are expecting a Savior, the Lord Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.21: He will transform the body of our humiliation that it may be conformed to the body of his glory, by the power that also enables him to make all things subject to himself.

NET.1: Finally, my brothers and sisters, rejoice in the Lord! To write this again is no trouble to me, and it is a safeguard for you.
NET.2: Beware of the dogs, beware of the evil workers, beware of those who mutilate the flesh!
NET.3: For we are the circumcision, the ones who worship by the Spirit of God, exult in Christ Jesus, and do not rely on human credentials
NET.4: — though mine too are significant. If someone thinks he has good reasons to put confidence in human credentials, I have more:
NET.5: I was circumcised on the eighth day, from the people of Israel and the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew of Hebrews. I lived according to the law as a Pharisee.
NET.6: In my zeal for God I persecuted the church. According to the righteousness stipulated in the law I was blameless.
NET.7: But these assets I have come to regard as liabilities because of Christ.
NET.8: More than that, I now regard all things as liabilities compared to the far greater value of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord, for whom I have suffered the loss of all things — indeed, I regard them as dung! — that I may gain Christ,
NET.9: and be found in him, not because I have my own righteousness derived from the law, but because I have the righteousness that comes by way of Christ’s faithfulness — a righteousness from God that is in fact based on Christ’s faithfulness.
NET.10: My aim is to know him, to experience the power of his resurrection, to share in his sufferings, and to be like him in his death,
NET.11: and so, somehow, to attain to the resurrection from the dead.
NET.12: Not that I have already attained this — that is, I have not already been perfected — but I strive to lay hold of that for which Christ Jesus also laid hold of me.
NET.13: Brothers and sisters, I do not consider myself to have attained this. Instead I am single-minded: Forgetting the things that are behind and reaching out for the things that are ahead,
NET.14: with this goal in mind, I strive toward the prize of the upward call of God in Christ Jesus.
NET.15: Therefore let those of us who are “perfect” embrace this point of view. If you think otherwise, God will reveal to you the error of your ways.
NET.16: Nevertheless, let us live up to the standard that we have already attained.
NET.17: Be imitators of me, brothers and sisters, and watch carefully those who are living this way, just as you have us as an example.
NET.18: For many live, about whom I have often told you, and now, with tears, I tell you that they are the enemies of the cross of Christ.
NET.19: Their end is destruction, their god is the belly, they exult in their shame, and they think about earthly things.
NET.20: But our citizenship is in heaven — and we also await a savior from there, the Lord Jesus Christ,
NET.21: who will transform these humble bodies of ours into the likeness of his glorious body by means of that power by which he is able to subject all things to himself.
